data.title: GSK Discloses July 22 Executive ADS Acquisition by U.S. President
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

GlaxoSmithKline ( (GSK) ) has issued an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, GSK notified the market of a personal dealing transaction by Maya Martinez-Davis, its President, US, involving the acquisition of notional American Depositary Shares through her Executive Supplemental Savings Plan account. The transaction, executed on the New York Stock Exchange at a price of $50.76 per ADS for a volume of 9.407 ADS, reflects standard participation by a senior U.S. executive in the company’s long-term savings and incentive arrangements.

The disclosure, formally filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on July 24, 2026, underscores GSK’s adherence to regulatory requirements governing transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities. While the scale of the acquisition is modest, such filings provide transparency for investors around executive-linked holdings in GSK’s U.S.-traded securities and reinforce governance practices expected of a major global biopharma issuer.

The most recent analyst rating on (GSK) stock is a Hold
with a $70.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock,
see the GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (strong margins and cash generation, though with leverage and variability) and supportive valuation (low P/E with a ~3.5% dividend). This is meaningfully offset by weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while the latest earnings call was broadly constructive on guidance and specialty-led momentum but highlighted H2 phasing and launch/access headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock,
click here.

More about GlaxoSmithKline

GSK plc is a global biopharma company focused on uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease. The group develops and markets prescription medicines and vaccines across major therapeutic areas, with a strong presence in the U.S. market and listings that include American Depositary Shares on the New York Stock Exchange.

Headquartered in London and registered in England and Wales, GSK operates as one of the leading international pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical issuers. Its securities reporting obligations include regular filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as a foreign private issuer under Form 20-F rules.

Average Trading Volume: 3,981,904

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $101.9B

For an in-depth examination of GSK stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Liberty Global Posts Solid Q2 Results, Expands Fiber Footprint
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Liberty Global A ( (LBTYA) ) is now available.

Liberty Global reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, highlighting solid operational performance across its European telecom businesses, including broadband subscriber gains at VodafoneZiggo and Telenet and improved trading at Virgin Media O2, alongside continued fiber network expansion to 9 million premises in the UK and sustained mobile and broadband growth in Ireland. The company also advanced preparations for a planned Ziggo Group spin-off in 2027, exited its remaining EdgeConneX stake for $604 million, lifted year-to-date asset monetizations to about $1.2 billion and raised its year-end corporate cash target to roughly $2.0 billion, underscoring a strategy of asset rotation, balance-sheet strengthening and shareholder value focus.

The most recent analyst rating on (LBTYA) stock is a Hold
with a $12.10 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Liberty Global A stock,
see the LBTYA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on LBTYA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LBTYA is a Neutral.

LBTYA scores around the middle primarily because financial performance is mixed—stable revenue and positive (but weakened) free cash flow are outweighed by large recent net losses and deteriorating returns/leverage. Technicals further pressure the score due to a clear downtrend across moving averages and negative MACD. Offsetting these risks, the latest earnings call was moderately positive with reaffirmed 2026 guidance, improving broadband trends, and progress on strategic value-unlock initiatives, while valuation remains hard to assess given negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on LBTYA stock,
click here.

More about Liberty Global A

Liberty Global Ltd. is an international telecommunications and media company with operations across Europe, including the Benelux region, the UK and Ireland. Through platforms such as VodafoneZiggo, Telenet, Virgin Media O2 and Virgin Media Ireland, it provides broadband, mobile and converged telecom services, and is increasingly focused on full fiber networks and infrastructure-driven growth.

Current Market Cap: $3.5B

data.title: OptimumBank Reports Record Second-Quarter 2026 Earnings Growth
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Optimumbank Holdings ( (OPHC) ) has provided an announcement.

OptimumBank Holdings, Inc. reported strong second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, 2026, delivering record net income of $6.7 million and net income of $11.3 million for the first half of 2026, driven by higher net interest income, rising noninterest income and disciplined funding costs. Returns on average assets and equity improved to 2.04% and 20.34%, respectively, while net interest margin rose to 4.57%, supported by robust loan growth, deposit expansion and careful balance sheet management.

Total assets reached about $1.4 billion at June 30, 2026, as gross loans and total deposits each climbed to roughly $1.2 billion, reflecting double-digit quarterly growth and a 38.1% increase in deposits year over year. Credit quality remained solid, with a modest credit loss reversal, low net charge-offs and an allowance for credit losses at 0.91% of total loans, while capital ratios stayed comfortably above regulatory minimums despite rapid asset growth.

During the quarter, the company completed a leadership transition effective May 1, 2026, as long-time chairman Moishe Gubin assumed the additional role of chief executive officer, Braden R. Smith became president and former CEO Timothy Terry retired but continued to assist with succession. Management emphasized continued execution of strategic growth initiatives, including expansion into complementary services via OptimumFinance, LLC, which closed its first $14.2 million loan funded by a $14.0 million note, enhancing yield and diversifying revenue.

Noninterest income rose to $2.5 million in the second quarter of 2026 on higher gains from SBA loan sales and increased fee income, while noninterest expenses climbed to $8.4 million mainly due to higher compensation costs, keeping the efficiency ratio under 50%. Funding trends were favorable, with strong growth across all deposit categories and a reduction in Federal Home Loan Bank advances, underscoring improved balance sheet flexibility and positioning the bank for ongoing earnings momentum.

The company’s expanding profile was underscored by recent external recognition, including a number 49 ranking out of 3,465 U.S. community banks by S&P Global Market Intelligence in the first quarter of 2026 and a top-10% placement in Raymond James’s Community Bankers Cup in the second quarter. These accolades, alongside new analyst coverage, signal rising visibility among investors and reinforce OptimumBank’s positioning as a high-performing community bank focused on sustained growth and long-term shareholder returns.

The most recent analyst rating on (OPHC) stock is a Buy
with a $6.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Optimumbank Holdings stock,
see the OPHC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on OPHC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, OPHC is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (profitability, improving leverage, and strengthening cash generation) and a very low P/E valuation. Technicals are constructive but not strongly bullish due to near-term consolidation, while recent corporate actions are positive and growth-oriented but carry execution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on OPHC stock,
click here.

More about Optimumbank Holdings

OptimumBank Holdings, Inc. is a financial holding company based in Fort Lauderdale that owns 100% of OptimumBank, a Florida-chartered commercial bank, as well as non-bank subsidiaries OptimumHUD Loans and OptimumFinance. The group focuses on relationship-based community banking, commercial real estate lending, government-guaranteed SBA loans and complementary financing services, with a growing presence in deposit gathering and specialized lending across its markets.

The company operates with a diversified platform that includes bridge and HUD lending through OptimumHUD Loans and newly launched activities at OptimumFinance, LLC, which began originating loans in the second quarter of 2026. Its strategy centers on disciplined loan and deposit pricing, balance sheet optimization and expansion into complementary financial services to support long-term growth and shareholder value.

Average Trading Volume: 27,329

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $74.59M

data.title: Dyadic Regains Nasdaq Compliance, Secures Continued Listing
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from Dyadic International ( (DYAI) ) is now available.

On July 24, 2026, Dyadic International announced that Nasdaq confirmed the company has regained compliance with Listing Rules 5550(a)(2) and 5550(b), allowing its common stock to remain listed and traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The resolution of the listing issue removes a key uncertainty around Dyadic’s market status and helps preserve access to public equity markets for its shareholders.

Chief executive Mark Emalfarb said the restored compliance provides greater stability as Dyadic continues executing its business strategy, including developing and commercializing its own products and expanding adoption of its C1 and Dapibus protein production platforms. The continued Nasdaq listing supports Dyadic’s efforts to advance strategic collaborations and underpins its goal of creating long-term value for investors while reinforcing its positioning in the competitive biotechnology sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (DYAI) stock is a Buy
with a $3.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dyadic International stock,
see the DYAI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DYAI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DYAI is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (large ongoing losses, negative cash flow, and negative equity with higher debt) and heightened corporate risk from Nasdaq delisting review. These are partially offset by strong technical momentum (price above major moving averages with positive MACD) and a constructive earnings update showing accelerating revenue and disciplined cash-burn guidance, though profitability and scaling remain unproven.

To see Spark’s full report on DYAI stock,
click here.

More about Dyadic International

Dyadic International, Inc., doing business as Dyadic Applied BioSolutions, is a global biotechnology company focused on developing and commercializing scalable, non-animal recombinant protein production platforms. Its proprietary C1 and Dapibus expression systems target applications across the life sciences, food and nutrition, bio-industrial and biopharmaceutical markets, enabling customers to produce high-value proteins more efficiently and sustainably.

Dyadic’s platforms are designed to support rapid, cost-effective and flexible manufacturing of proteins to meet growing global demand in these sectors. The company positions itself as a provider of next-generation production technologies aimed at improving scalability, reducing costs and enhancing sustainability for partners and customers worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 333,492

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $47.37M

On 24 July 2026, GSK plc reported that 70,759 additional ordinary shares of 31¼ pence each were admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange’s Main Market under its existing block listing. The new shares, issued in connection with the GlaxoSmithKline plc Share Save Plan 2022, are fully fungible with the company’s existing stock, bringing the total number of ordinary shares in issue and admitted to trading to 4,316,292,310.

The admission, covering share issues between 29 May 2026 and 24 July 2026, reflects ongoing employee participation in GSK’s savings-related share purchase scheme and results in modest dilution for existing shareholders. An update from Provident Financial Holdings ( (PROV) ) is now available.

Provident Financial Holdings, Inc., the parent of Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., announced that President and Chief Executive Officer Donavon P. Ternes will join the Boards of Directors of both the corporation and the bank beginning July 23, 2026, filling vacancies created by the death of Director William E. Thomas in April 2026. Ternes, who has served as CEO since January 2024 and has held progressively senior roles since joining in 2000, will serve on the boards until the corporation’s 2027 and the bank’s 2026 annual stockholder meetings.

The bank also appointed Michael S. Van Stockum, age 63, as Senior Vice President and Chief Lending Officer effective July 23, 2026, reflecting a consolidation of experienced leadership in its lending operations. Van Stockum, who has more than 40 years of lending and credit management experience and has been with the bank since 2007 in roles including Commercial Real Estate Loan Administrator and Community Reinvestment Act Officer, has no reportable related-party transactions or family relationships with current directors or executives, underscoring governance and compliance considerations for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (PROV) stock is a Hold
with a $17.00 price target.

On July 23, 2026, Gorman-Rupp announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer James C. Kerr plans to retire, having served as CFO since 2017 after joining the company in 2016. Kerr, credited with elevating the firm’s finance, IT, strategic planning, investor relations, and acquisition capabilities, will step down as CFO on October 1, 2026, then remain as a senior advisor until a later retirement date.

The company named current Vice President of Finance, 41-year-old Ronald F. Stoops, as Kerr’s successor effective October 1, 2026, formalizing the transition in a July 24, 2026 press release. Stoops, who joined Gorman-Rupp in 2020 and has led finance since January 2025, brings experience in financial reporting, tax compliance, strategic planning, and prior roles at a global manufacturer and KPMG, with the company signaling a continuity-focused succession free of related-party or conflict-of-interest concerns.

Sidus Space, a space and defense technology company focused on dual-use satellite manufacturing, AI-driven space-based data solutions, mission planning services, and space and defense hardware, operates from Florida’s Space Coast with a 35,000-square-foot integration and testing facility. The company leverages its LizzieSat satellite and sensor system to support government, defense, intelligence, and commercial clients worldwide with flexible, cost-effective space solutions.

On July 24, 2026, Sidus Space announced the appointment of Alan Khalili as Chief Financial Officer, effective July 27, 2026, succeeding interim CFO John Burke during the company’s transition period. With over two decades of financial leadership in space, satellite, and technology sectors, Khalili is expected to lead all financial operations and support Sidus’s next phase of growth, reinforcing its commercialization, satellite manufacturing expansion, AI and digital mission computing platforms, and drive toward durable, recurring revenue across government and commercial markets.

Headquartered on Florida’s Space Coast, it operates a 35,000-square-foot manufacturing, assembly, integration, and testing facility and serves government, defense, intelligence, and commercial customers globally, leveraging its LizzieSat satellite and sensor system to deliver flexible, cost-effective space solutions.

Verizon ( (VZ) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Verizon Communications Inc. amended the employment agreement of Chief Executive Officer Daniel H. Schulman, extending the initial term of his contract by one year to December 31, 2028, with automatic one-year renewals thereafter unless either party provides at least 90 days’ notice. For calendar year 2028, Schulman will receive no less than his 2027 base salary and target annual incentive, plus a sole long‑term incentive award with a target value of at least $25 million granted in early 2027 on terms aligned with other top executives, with vesting fully accelerated if his employment ends due to a defined succession event.

The amendment also provides that Schulman’s compensation beyond 2028 will be set by the board in line with decisions for Verizon’s most senior executives, underscoring the board’s intent to retain flexibility over long‑term pay while signaling confidence in his leadership through 2028. The clarified structure of salary, incentives, and succession‑related vesting protections may influence investor perception of Verizon’s leadership stability and succession planning, while highlighting the company’s continued reliance on high-value, performance‑linked pay to align executive interests with long‑term corporate goals.

Surgery Partners ( (SGRY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 21, 2026, Surgery Partners and Intermountain Health placed into escrow signed agreements under which Intermountain would acquire Surgery Partners’ ownership interests in Mountain View Hospital and Idaho Falls Community Hospital, valuing the Idaho Falls facilities at about $1.15 billion and providing approximately $795 million in consideration to Surgery Partners, subject to customary adjustments. The transaction, announced publicly on July 24, 2026, marks a major step in Surgery Partners’ portfolio optimization strategy, is contingent on physician approvals, regulatory clearances and other closing conditions, preserves existing physician ownership of Mountain View Hospital, and supports the company’s strategic shift toward ambulatory surgery centers while it reaffirms its 2026 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA outlook excluding the deal’s impact.

The sale involves hospital operations in Idaho Falls that include 126 beds, more than 150 physicians and a broad mix of surgical and acute care services, with Intermountain positioned as the new owner alongside longstanding physician partners. Company leadership framed the divestiture as simplifying ongoing operations, sharpening strategic focus and potentially enhancing long-term shareholder value, while warning that completion is not assured until all approvals are secured and noting that final net cash proceeds cannot yet be reasonably estimated.

Tompkins Financial Corporation reported record earnings for the third consecutive quarter, with diluted earnings per share reaching $2.04 in the second quarter of 2026, a 36% increase from a year earlier and 12.1% higher than the first quarter. Net income for the quarter rose to $29.3 million, while first-half 2026 net income climbed 34.6% year over year to $55.4 million, driven by higher net interest income, stronger net interest margin and solid loan growth.

Net interest income grew 23% year over year in the second quarter to $74 million as net interest margin widened to 3.58%, supported by higher yields on earning assets and controlled funding costs, while average loans and deposits both expanded versus 2025. On July 22, 2026, System1 held its annual meeting, where shareholders approved the issuance of Series A Cumulative Convertible Preferred Stock, elected three Class I directors for terms expiring in 2029 and ratified Deloitte and Touche LLP as the company’s independent auditor for the 2026 fiscal year. data.title: KULR Sells Bitcoin Holdings to Eliminate Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KULR Technology Group ( (KULR) ).

During the period from July 9 to July 23, 2026, KULR Technology Group, Inc. sold approximately 333 bitcoin in open-market transactions at a weighted average price of about $64,538 per BTC, generating roughly $21.5 million in gross proceeds. The sales were part of the company’s treasury management strategy and were completed against a backdrop of heightened market volatility.

KULR used the net proceeds to fully repay its $20 million credit facility with Coinbase Credit, leaving no principal outstanding and positioning the company with a largely debt-free balance sheet. With all 565 BTC previously pledged as collateral expected to be returned and approximately 760 BTC still held as of July 23, 2026, the move is intended to cut interest expense, reduce collateral and liquidation risk, and increase financial flexibility under its newly appointed Board and CFO.

The transaction underscores KULR’s disciplined approach to capital allocation and risk management, balancing continued exposure to bitcoin’s potential upside with a stronger financial foundation. Management framed the decision as a proactive step to prioritize stockholder value while the company continues to scale its core battery technology operations.

Spark’s Take on KULR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KULR is a Neutral.

The score is held back most by weak financial performance—large ongoing losses and heavy negative free cash flow—despite solid revenue growth and low current leverage. Offsetting this, the earnings call points to improving commercial traction and a substantial gross-margin step-up with continued cost discipline, though execution risk around the planned H2 production ramp remains high. Technically, the stock shows a mixed picture with longer-term support above key moving averages but muted near-term momentum, while valuation lacks support given negative earnings and no dividend.
